intro: Pseudohemipilia leucotricha, also known as Himalayan snake root, is a plant species native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Icacinaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudohemipilia leucotricha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or powder, and is also used in the form of herbal teas, capsules, and extracts to harness its health benefits.
